column1: II. Functional Requirements (What Does the System Need to Do?)

column2: Fast and accurate search capabilities by keywords, skills, salary, job title, candidate status, Name, departure date, arrival date, employer.
System should suggest the best candidates for a specific job order based on skills and criteria.

Generate reports on: Placement rates, Time-to-Hire, Active Candidates, Client performance, Revenue/Commission tracking.
Employer attrition rate
Track the progression of candidates through the hiring pipeline (e.g., Applied -> Screened -> Interviewed -> Placed).
Ability to update the status of multiple candidates or send bulk communications (if integrated with email).

Define different user levels (e.g., Administrator, Recruiter, Management) with specific permissions.
Administrator - Access to everything and source code
Management - Access to everything, no code access
Recruiter - Access to create, edit, delete, upload records
Ensure mechanisms for consent, data access, and data deletion are in place. Crucial for sensitive data.
Data must be encrypted (at rest and in transit) and protected by strong authentication.
Log all changes and actions performed by users (who did what and when) for accountability.
Implement a regular, reliable process for backing up all data, with a clear recovery plan.
The database must be able to handle future growth in the number of candidates and clients (e.g., scaling from 5,000 to 50,000 records).
The system needs to be consistently available during business hours (7AM-6PM)
The interface should be intuitive and easy for users to use daily, minimizing training time.
Potential integration with existing tools (e.g., Email clients, SMS gateways, Accounting software).
column3: Pati si OFWs pwede din iemail via group (dipende sa kung sino employer nila, or kung ano job nila) based on sets or kung sino kasama nila

column2: A plan for migrating any existing data (spreadsheets, old databases) into the new system.
